hero.title: Producing is not enough: you need to be connected.
hero.subtitle: In Togo, thousands of farms remain disconnected from markets, services, and cooperative networks. Explore investment opportunities in agricultural service white zones.

act1.title: Where is production happening?
act1.subtitle: Agricultural production basins
act1.body: Togo has thousands of farms spread across the country. The Centrale and Kara regions concentrate a significant share of production, while Plateaux and Maritime show strong potential. This density map reveals the production basins where agricultural activity is most intense.
act1.key_finding: Centrale and Kara regions concentrate 45% of Togo's farms
act1.question: Do these production areas have access to essential services?

act2.title: Is it developed?
act2.subtitle: Coverage of Planned Agricultural Development Zones (ZAAP)
act2.body: ZAAPs are designed to structure and support agricultural production with adapted infrastructure. Yet many production basins are not covered by a ZAAP within a 5 km radius. These uncovered areas represent major investment opportunities for network expansion.
act2.key_finding: Major production basins are not covered by any ZAAP
act2.question: How can we connect these isolated areas to markets?

act3.title: Is it accessible?
act3.subtitle: Access to markets and nurseries
act3.body: Proximity to markets and nurseries is crucial for farm commercialization and supply. Production areas more than 10 km from a market or 15 km from a nursery are considered underserved, limiting their economic development.
act3.key_finding: Several production areas are more than 10 km from a market
act3.question: Are producers organized to access services together?

act4.title: Is it organized?
act4.subtitle: The cooperative network
act4.body: Agricultural cooperatives allow small producers to pool resources, access inputs, and negotiate collectively. Production areas without a cooperative within 10 km are 'organizational white zones' where cooperative potential remains to be developed.
act4.key_finding: Organizational white zones persist in high-potential regions
act4.question: Where to invest first for maximum impact?

synthesis.title: Where to invest?
synthesis.subtitle: Synthesis and priority recommendations
synthesis.body: The weighted overlay of 4 analyses — density, ZAAP coverage, accessibility, and cooperative network — reveals where investment will have the greatest impact. Composite scores identify priority regions for each type of intervention.
synthesis.rec_1_title: Expand the ZAAP network in Centrale region
synthesis.rec_1_description: Major production basin with insufficient ZAAP coverage. Priority: extend ZAAPs to cover undeveloped high-density areas.
synthesis.rec_1_action: Plan ZAAP extension and validate sites through field missions.
synthesis.rec_2_title: Create market access hubs in Kara region
synthesis.rec_2_description: High-density agricultural areas far from markets. Solution: mobile rural markets or collection hubs to be created.
synthesis.rec_2_action: Create collection points and pilot mobile rural markets.
synthesis.rec_3_title: Support cooperative networks in Plateaux region
synthesis.rec_3_description: High agricultural potential region with few cooperatives. Invest in cooperative organization and shared infrastructure.
synthesis.rec_3_action: Support cooperative creation and shared equipment.
synthesis.rec_3_impact: Strengthen bargaining power and access to inputs.

story.share_message: Discover agricultural service white zones in Togo — Where to invest first?
